other_observers: Kara Lewantowicz, Bill Maynard, Van Truan.; Found by David Silverman on June 19, 2006. Also, seen by Mark Peterson later in the day on July 8, 2006.
description: Two meadowlarks were a lot of white in the tail, and the face. The male giving call mentioned above. It looks like a pair, with a nest. Photos are attached of the male and female.Behaviors: The male was first noticed on a juniper, then a fence post, and then in the grass. The female was mostly seen in the grass, with food in its mouth.Call: The male gave a buzzy "bzeert" call many times. The female didn't make any noise.Plumage: adult male and female
photographer: Photographer: Brandon Percival, Bill Maynard, Bill Maynard
